q1.question: How does MyConciergeHotel curate its hotels?
q1.answer: We apply a single editorial grid: official distinctions (Atout France Palace label, Forbes Five Star, Michelin Keys), membership of author-led collections (Relais & Châteaux, Leading Hotels of the World), and an internal score authored by our concierge desk on service, signature and location. Every page is hand-written, never generated. No pay-to-play: no hotel commission influences the selection.

q2.question: How are you different from Booking or Hotels.com?
q2.answer: MyConciergeHotel is an IATA agency, not a price comparison engine. Our catalogue is curated to 2,193 addresses against hundreds of thousands, and every page is written by our concierge desk — not by the hotels themselves. No fabricated urgency, no struck-through prices, no « only X rooms left » banner. The price displayed in Phase 6 will be the Amadeus GDS net rate, with no agency surcharge.

q3.question: What is the Concierge's Tip at the bottom of each page?
q3.answer: A concrete operational secret — a corner room to ask for by number, a table to reserve three months ahead, an access route that changes your arrival. Our hotelier partners trusted us with it; we share it free of charge. It's what separates a fine stay from a memorable one.

q4.question: Is Le Concierge Club actually free?
q4.answer: Yes. The free Club is a one-minute signup, no credit card, no commitment. It opens the concierge agenda, your saved shortlist, and the operational tips of the Palaces we selected. The Prestige tier (€99/year, planned for Phase 6) is invitation-only and waitlist-based — it complements the free Club without replacing it.

openingsPage.faqTitle: Frequently asked questions about Concierge openings and visits

faqItems.q: How does the Concierge select the hotels that appear on this page?
faqItems.a: Our advisers personally visit the hotels listed, or rely on the Concierge inspector network — Atout France for the Palace label, the Michelin Guide for the Keys, Forbes Travel Guide, Relais & Châteaux and Leading Hotels of the World. No hotel pays to be listed here. The twenty addresses on display are the most recent picks — the list rotates as soon as a new visit is validated.

faqItems.q: Which palaces or Relais & Châteaux feature among the recent openings?
faqItems.a: Our catalogue covers 2,193 extraordinary hotels in 127 countries, including 434 Relais & Châteaux, 197 Small Luxury Hotels, 127 World 50 Best and 18 Atout France Palaces. This page highlights houses that have reopened after renovation, changed chef, earned a Michelin Key or a Forbes Five Star, or that we have just visited. Current examples include Le Bristol Paris, Akelarre (Spanish Basque Country), Al Moudira (Luxor), Alila Jabal Akhdar (Oman).

faqItems.q: Can I book a recently opened hotel through the Concierge?
faqItems.a: Yes for every address listed in our catalogue. You can browse the page, request a personalised quote via the concierge form (reply within 24 business hours), or — once Phase 6 of the project ships — book directly at net GDS rates through our IATA licence. Most bookings today flow through our concierge desk so guests benefit from upgrades, transfers and special requests negotiated directly with each property.

faqItems.q: How often is this page updated?
faqItems.a: The list is regenerated hourly server-side (Next.js ISR) from our concierge database. A new hotel appears as soon as its page is published and a visit has been validated by an adviser. Hotels move up or down according to the editorial priority adjusted by our editors.

faqItems.q: What is a 'validated' visit by your concierge desk?
faqItems.a: One of our advisers has spent at least one night on site, tested breakfast, the spa, two restaurants if available, and met the head concierge. When a direct visit is not possible, the hotel must combine at least two external authority signals (Atout France Palace, Michelin Key, Forbes Five Star, Relais & Châteaux, Leading Hotels of the World) and a photo plus press audit. No copy-paste from Booking or TripAdvisor.

faqItems.q: Do recently opened hotels cost more than an established address?
faqItems.a: Not systematically. Openings often benefit from launch rates ('opening rates') 10–20 % below catalogue, or even free nights during pre-opening. Our concierge desk negotiates these conditions directly when possible. Conversely, some historic Palaces charge more after a heavy renovation (Hôtel de Crillon, Lutetia).

faqItems.q: What does 'opening' mean on this page?
faqItems.a: Several cases: a new build (a hotel that did not exist before), an editorial re-opening (a hotel reopening after heavy renovation, under a new brand or new chef), or a first visit by our concierge desk (an established hotel we have just inspected and added to the catalogue). The context is spelt out in the Concierge's Tip at the bottom of each page.

faqItems.q: Do you cover openings outside France and Europe?
faqItems.a: Yes. Our 2026 catalogue spans 127 countries — flagship openings include Asia (Aman Tokyo, Capella Bangkok, Bvlgari Dubai), the Middle East, Africa (Al Moudira in Luxor, Singita lodges), Latin America (Riviera Maya, Patagonia), the Caribbean and the Indian Ocean. We favour signature houses over mass-market chains, wherever they are.

faqItems.q: How can I be notified about upcoming Concierge openings?
faqItems.a: Our newsletter Le Concierge — a single monthly issue, no commercial tracking — announces notable openings and closures, major renovations and new Michelin Keys or Forbes Five Star awards. Free subscription via the Newsletter page. You can also follow individual hotels from your account (favourites) to receive an alert when a major update lands.

faqItems.q: Does this list replace a ranking or a guide?
faqItems.a: No. This page is a chronological feed of the latest visits — it is the logbook of our concierge desk. To compare, rank or choose, consult our editorial rankings ('Best Palaces of France', 'Best hotel spas', etc.) or the destination guides (Paris, French Riviera, Italy, Japan…). The three surfaces complement each other.

itineraires.aeoQuestion: How do MyConciergeHotel bespoke itineraries work?
itineraires.aeoAnswer: Our editorial itineraries assemble 3 to 14 days of named stops, each night at a hotel curated by our IATA concierge desk — Palace, 5-star or signature house. Every itinerary details tested timings, local gastronomy, transfers and direct contacts with on-site Concierges. Editorial selection, never commercial.

faqItems.q: How long are MyConciergeHotel itineraries?
faqItems.a: Our itineraries span 3 days (signature weekend) to 14 days (grand Mediterranean or Alpine tour). Each page lists the min/max duration and clarifies which stops can be compressed or extended — our concierge desk can adapt on request without breaking editorial consistency.

faqItems.q: Are the recommended hotels bookable via MyConciergeHotel?
faqItems.a: Yes. Each stop links to the matching hotel page, bookable at Amadeus GDS net rates through our IATA licence. You can book each night individually or ask the concierge desk for a global quote including transfers, upgrades and special requests.

faqItems.q: Can I customise an itinerary to fit my trip?
faqItems.a: The published itinerary is an editorial framework. Our concierge adjusts durations, swaps a stop, adds a gastronomic detour or substitutes a hotel based on your constraints — dates, budget, mobility, family travel. A personalised quote takes 48 hours.

faqItems.q: Do itineraries include transfers and chauffeurs?
faqItems.a: Itinerary pages detail transport options (high-speed rail, domestic flight, car rental, private driver) with indicative durations and our recommended partners. Chauffeur services are handled by our Concierge network, sourced locally and invoiced separately from the hotel rate.

faqItems.q: Do itineraries cover destinations outside France?
faqItems.a: Yes. Our 2026 editorial catalog includes Italy (Tuscany, Lakes, Sicily), Morocco (Marrakech-Atlas), Switzerland (Alps-Lake Geneva), Japan (Tokyo-Kyoto), Maldives, UAE. Every non-French itinerary follows the same editorial grid: named stops, signature hotels, tested timings.

inspiration.aeoQuestion: How do you find the right hotel for your trip on MyConciergeHotel?
inspiration.aeoAnswer: MyConciergeHotel aggregates its Palaces and 5-star hotels across three complementary axes: 12 themes (romantic, spa, gastronomy, design, vineyards, heritage, golf, ski, pool, rooftop, kids-friendly), 9 travel occasions (honeymoon, wedding, seminar, anniversary, wellness retreat) and 4 seasons. Every combination opens an editorial sub-hub with a selection curated by our concierge desk. Last updated {date}.

faqItems.q: What's the difference between a theme, an occasion and a season?
faqItems.a: A theme describes the main experience sought (spa, gastronomy, design) — it characterises the hotel. An occasion describes the event motivating the trip (honeymoon, wedding, seminar) — it describes the traveller. A season describes the relevant time window (winter in the mountains, summer by the sea) — it describes the calendar. The three axes cross: a hotel can be romantic (theme), ideal for a honeymoon (occasion) and at its best in spring (season).

faqItems.q: Why 12 themes rather than 50?
faqItems.a: We cap at 12 themes to guarantee each axis has at least 5 published addresses — a theme with a single page has no value for the user or for AEO. Relevant sub-themes (Michelin 2-star, spa with private sauna) are search-engine sub-facets, not standalone hubs.

faqItems.q: How is inspiration linked to the rankings?
faqItems.a: Each theme, occasion or season opens `/classements/{axis}/{value}` — a matrix sub-hub listing every editorial ranking that carries that axis. For example "Spa & wellness" leads to the "Best hotel spas", "Palaces with hammam", "Wellness retreats" rankings published on that theme.

faqItems.q: A season without a destination — what does it return?
faqItems.a: A "Winter" ranking without a listed destination means we cover the winter experience (ski, après-ski, spa) across all zones — Alps, Pyrenees, but also French Riviera off-season. A "Winter in Courchevel" ranking is more specific and appears in the `/classements/lieu/courchevel` sub-hub.

faqItems.q: Why not a "budget" axis?
faqItems.a: All our hotels are Palace or 5-star — the price segment is premium by construction. A "budget" axis would have no useful granularity here; we do however offer a non-affiliated price comparator on every page to compare with aggregators.

faqItems.q: When are new inspirations published?
faqItems.a: The taxonomy is frozen quarterly (ranking consistency). New themes can appear if the concierge desk publishes 5+ dedicated rankings (e.g. "luxury surf" could open if we publish 5 seaside × surf rankings).

brands.aeoQuestion: Which hotel groups does MyConciergeHotel represent in its catalogue?
brands.aeoAnswer: MyConciergeHotel covers {count} author hotel collections worldwide — Aman, Belmond, Six Senses, Bulgari, Mandarin Oriental, Four Seasons, Rosewood, Park Hyatt, Raffles, The Peninsula, Shangri-La, Oetker Collection (Le Bristol, Hôtel du Cap-Eden-Roc), Dorchester Collection (Le Meurice, Plaza Athénée), Cheval Blanc, Airelles, Les K2, Caudalie. Iconic independents (Negresco, Lutetia, Crillon, Villa La Coste, Reid's Palace) are also listed without chain affiliation. Last updated {date}.

faqItems.q: Are you affiliated with or paid by these hotel groups?
faqItems.a: No. MyConciergeHotel is an independent IATA-accredited agency. No brand pays to be listed or featured. Our commission comes from bookings (IATA standard), identical from one brand to another — the selection is strictly editorial.

faqItems.q: Which major international houses are in the catalogue?
faqItems.a: Our catalogue covers the leading author collections: Aman (Asia, Americas, Europe), Belmond (Italy, Latin America, Venice Simplon Orient-Express), Six Senses (Bali, Maldives, Switzerland, Portugal), Bulgari (Milan, Dubai, Tokyo, Paris), Mandarin Oriental, Four Seasons, Rosewood, Park Hyatt, Raffles, The Peninsula, Shangri-La. New openings are published on /ouvertures as soon as the visit is validated by our concierge desk.

faqItems.q: How does a brand join your editorial catalogue?
faqItems.a: We select hotel groups on three criteria: strong editorial signature (author collection, not a marketing banner), verifiable distinctions (Forbes Five Star, Michelin Keys, Relais & Châteaux, Leading Hotels of the World), Amadeus GDS connectivity for direct booking. A brand doesn't apply — our concierge desk proposes.

faqItems.q: What does "collection" vs "chain" mean in your catalogue?
faqItems.a: A collection (Oetker, Dorchester, Cheval Blanc) groups a few author addresses, often unique by architecture or history. A chain (Four Seasons, Mandarin Oriental) deploys a service standard at scale while maintaining a high level. Both feature in the catalogue; our editorial tends to favour collections for their signature.

faqItems.q: Can I earn the brand's loyalty points by booking through you?
faqItems.a: Yes — your loyalty number (Four Seasons Preferred, Marriott Bonvoy for Ritz-Carlton…) can be attached to the booking. Points are credited by the hotel after the stay. Our MyConciergeHotel programme stacks on top of the in-house programmes without replacing them.

faqItems.q: How do I know if a specific hotel belongs to one of these brands?
faqItems.a: The hotel page prominently mentions collection membership (Palace + group as a chip under the H1). If the brand is ambiguous (recent acquisition, sub-brand), we clarify the history in the "About the property" section.
